content:
===accordion
===panel: Intro

===endpanel
===endaccordion

[[Baylen, the Haymaker]] is a combo deck that is trying to create infinite tokens as fast as possible.  This can be any token, not just creatures.  With these tokens We can generate infinite mana in all colors and draw our whole deck with Baylen's first and second ability.  At this point we can assemble a win in a variety of ways depending on board state.  We are running a light stax package and a good amount of permanent removal.  If you choose to move to combat and swing out with a trillion [[Felidar Guardian]] or [[Dualcaster Mage]], with your deck in hand you are able to play all of your protection pieces and silence effects so you can move to combat worry free.  


===accordion
===panel: Infinite Token Combos

===endpanel
===endaccordion

[[Dockside Extortionist]] + [[Emiel the Blessed]] or [[Temur Sabertooth]]:  This is a simple combo where you are going to flicker [[Dockside Extortionist]] With [[Emiel the Blessed]] and create infinite treasure tokens.  This combo works as long as [[Dockside Extortionist]] can make 4 treasures. You can do a similar combo by returning [[Dockside Extortionist]] to your hand and recasting repeatedly with [[Temur Sabretooth]].  This combo will require [[Dockside Extortionist]] to make 5 treasures.  It's worth noting that [[Emiel the Blessed]] is an instant speed flicker that requires no casting, as opposed to [[Temur Sabertooth]] which will require you to continually cast [[Dockside Extortionist]].  This is also true with [[Cloudstone Curio]]. Once you have infinite treasure tokens you can now draw your deck.  An easy win from here is to cast [[Voldaren Epicure]] and loop it with either [[Emiel the Blessed]] or [[Temur Sabertooth]]. 


[[Dualcaster Mage]] + [[Twinflame]]:  Target any creature you control with [[Twinflame]].  With [[Twinflame]] on the stack, flash in [[Dualcaster Mage]] targeting [[Twinflame]]. Continue this process of copying [[Twinflame]] with the [[Dualcaster Mage]] ETB's to create infinite [[Dualcaster Mage]] Tokens with haste.  You can now either move to combat and swing out or create infinite mana and draw your deck.  I would recommend drawing your deck and playing [[Grand Abolisher]] and [[Sigarda, Font of Blessings]]. You can also give [[Sigarda, Font of Blessings]] hex proof with something like [[Legolas's Quick Reflexes]] to avoid channel abilities.  Was that last part overkill? Yes, but why not.  Honestly just tell the table that you are going to do that and they probably just scoop.


[[Kiki-Jiki, Mirror Breaker]] + [[Felidar Guardian]]:  With both of these permanents on the battlefield you can activate [[Kiki-Jiki, Mirror Breaker]] targeting [[Felidar Guardian]].  This will untap [[Kiki-Jiki, Mirror Breaker]] and now you can present a loop of creating infinite [[Feldar Guardian]] with haste.  Let's talk about the best way to assemble this combo.  With [[Birthing Pod]] on the battlefield you can activate it and sac any 3 drop.  Like [[Baylen, the Haymaker]] for example.  from hear you can tutor [[Felidar Guardian]] straight to the battlefield.  On ETB have it flicker [[Birthing Pod]].  Now you can reactivate it and sac [[Felidar Guardian]] to go get [[Karmic Guide]].  On ETB bring [[Felidar Guardian]] back to the battlefield flickering [[Birthing Pod]].  You will once again sac [[Felidar Guardian]] and this time tutor [[Kiki-Jiki, Mirror Breaker]] to the battlefield.  Activate him to make a token of [[Karmic Guide]].  The token will bring back [[Felidar Guardian]] to the battlefield and now you can combo off.  


[[Village Bell-Ringer]] + [[Kiki-Jiki, Mirror Breaker]]:  Similar to [[Felidar Guardian]] you can make infinite tokens of [[Village Bell-Ringer]] because every token made untaps [[Kiki-jiki, Mirror Breaker]].  Its also worth noting that [[Village Bell-Ringer]] can save you from a card like [[Blind Obedience]].  You can make your infinite tapped tokens then cast [[Village Bell-Ringer]].  Now you can access your whole deck with infinite mana.  

[[Cloudstone Curio]] + [[Dockside Extortionist]]:  This will also require another creature.  Preferably a 1 drop so [[Dockside Extortionist]] will only need to be able to make four treasure tokens to make it go infinite.  With [[Cloudstone Curiio]] on the battlefield, cast [[Dockside Extortionist]].  When it enters the battlefield return the other creature to hand and repeat this process for infinite treasure tokens.  If you do this loop with [[Dockside Extortionist]] and [[Voldaren Epicure]] it will just kill the table.  


===accordion
===panel: Other Win Cons

===endpanel
===endaccordion

[[Eternal Witness]] + [[Lightning Bolt]]:  This combo will also require a lot or infinite mana and either [[Emiel the Blessed]] or [[Temur Sabertooth]] to flicker or put back to hand [[Eternal Witness]].  With one of the two previously mentioned creatures and [[Eternal Witness]] on the battlefield, cast [[Lightning Bolt]] targeting an opponent. Flicker [[Eternal Witness]] to bring [[Lightning Bolt]] back to hand. Repeat this process until the table is dead.  

[[Blind Obedience]] + [[Temur Sabertooth]]:  This will also require a lot or infinite mana.  This is easiest with [[Dockside Extortionist]].  Every time you recast the creature after returning it to hand with [[Temur Sabertooth]], pay [[symbol:w]]. this will deal damage to each opponent and gain you life.  Do this enough times to kill the table. 


===accordion
===panel: Passive Token Generators

===endpanel
===endaccordion

[[Smothering Tithe]]:  This is obviously a great card for generating mana.  It's even better in this deck.  as you make treasures with the heavy card draw in todays CEDH meta you are able to tap them down and draw cards or make mana without sacking your tokens.  they can be used again for this or just crack them as per usual.  [[Baylen, the Haymaker]] really lets you abuse treasures. 

[[Arasta of the Endless Web]]:  This card will make you a lot of creature tokens even in just 1 turn cycle. One counter war between two other opponents can leave you with six spiders.  That's three mana or two card draws.  Again these tokens can just be tapped for ramp and card draw.  I am on [[Crop Rotation]] and [[Emergence Zone]] just in case i draw into a win on somebody else's turn.  As long as you can crack [[Emergence Zone]] you can win on any turn with the [[Blind Obedience]] line, The  [[Lightning Bolt]] line or by looping [[Voldaren Epicure]].   

[[Charismatic Conqueror]]:  Just like [[Arasta of the Endless Web]] this can make you a lot of valuable creature tokens.  It also doubles as a stax piece.  If i make a token, great.  If they bring there mana rocks or creatures in tapped, also great.  


  
===accordion
===panel: Closing

===endpanel
===endaccordion

This is just a quick description on how the deck works.  I am going to play test this deck and bring it to tournaments.  I will get more into detail as i learn more about the deck.  I may remove some of these combos and try new ones like [[Rosie Cotton of South Lane]] + [[Scurry Oak]].  We shall see.
